long_stay: Advanced Technician’s Certificate (Brevet de Technicien Supérieur – BTS)
Programs at accredited private higher education institutions
qualifying_courses.notes: Must be a full-time course of study leading to a higher education qualification. Part-time or distance study generally does not qualify.

types.name: Long-stay Study Visa
types.notes: Requires prior approval of a Temporary Authorisation to Stay (AST) from the Directorate General of Immigration.

booking_methods.description: For the initial Temporary Authorisation to Stay (AST) application from abroad.
For Type D visa application after AST approval. Booking method varies by country.

steps.title: Apply for Temporary Authorisation to Stay (AST)
steps.description: Submit a request for temporary authorisation to stay to the Directorate General of Immigration or a diplomatic/consular representation from your country of origin, before entering Luxembourg.
steps.action_required: Apply via post or MyGuichet.lu portal.

steps.title: Apply for Type D Visa (if required)
steps.description: After AST approval, if you are subject to a visa requirement to enter Luxembourg, apply for a Type D visa at the nearest Luxembourg embassy/consulate.
steps.action_required: Contact embassy/consulate to book an appointment and submit visa application.

steps.title: Enter Luxembourg
steps.description: The temporary residence authorisation is valid for 90 days, within which you must enter Luxembourg.

steps.title: Declare Arrival at Commune
steps.description: Within 3 days of arriving in Luxembourg, register at the local commune administration (Administration Communale) where you live.

steps.title: Undergo Medical Check
steps.description: Undergo a medical examination by a certified general practitioner in Luxembourg and clear a mandatory tuberculosis screening.
steps.action_required: Schedule and complete medical examination and screening.

steps.title: Apply for Residence Permit
steps.description: Within 3 months of arrival, submit your final application for a 'Residence Permit for Students' to the Directorate of Immigration.
steps.action_required: Submit application online via MyGuichet.lu or by post.

required_documents.name: Valid Passport
required_documents.details: Certified full copy (all pages), valid for at least 3 months beyond intended stay.

required_documents.name: Letter of Admission
required_documents.details: Official letter of admission from an accredited Luxembourg higher education institution.

required_documents.name: Proof of Sufficient Financial Resources
required_documents.details: Documentation proving access to required monthly funds.

required_documents.name: Proof of Comprehensive Health Insurance
required_documents.details: Covering the full period of travel and stay in Luxembourg.

required_documents.name: Copy of Residence Permit from another EU Member State
required_documents.details: Where applicable, if residing in another EU member state.

required_documents.name: Parental Consent
required_documents.details: For minors applying for a study visa.

financial_requirements.reference_basis: 80% of the current social inclusion income (REVIS) in Luxembourg
acceptable_proof: Original certificate of a student grant or scholarship
Bank certificate confirming funds are held in your name for the last 6 months
Signed sponsorship declaration (attestation de prise en charge) from parents or a guarantor legally living in Luxembourg
Income from salaried activity (maximum 15 hours per week)

residence_permit.authority_full_name: Directorate General of Immigration of the Ministry of Home Affairs
required_documents: Proof of admission to a higher education institution
residence_permit.notes: Declaration of arrival at the local commune required within 3 days of arrival. A medical examination and tuberculosis screening are mandatory.

restrictions: Bachelor's and BTS students are not permitted to work during their first two semesters of study.
work_while_studying.notes: Work hours are calculated as a monthly average, not a strict weekly cap (maximum 60 hours over a 4-week period).

required_documents: Application form (Demande en renouvellement d'un titre de séjour pour étudiant)
permit_renewal.notes: Must meet original conditions for enrollment and financial resources. Application must be submitted before the current permit expires.

requirements: Must have successfully completed a Master's degree or defended a doctoral thesis in Luxembourg
Application must be made at least 30 days before the expiry date of the student residence permit
Proof of monthly financial resources of at least €1,161.60
post_study_work.notes: This pathway is explicitly for Master's and PhD-level graduates. Bachelor's graduates have more limited options.

official_sources.name: Guichet.lu (The Administrative Guide of Luxembourg)
official_sources.description: Official Luxembourg government citizen-services portal, including immigration section for residence permits.

official_sources.name: Ministry of Home Affairs / Directorate General of Immigration
University of Luxembourg Immigration Portal
official_sources.description: Information for international students at the University of Luxembourg.

application_forms.name: Engagement de prise en charge (financial sponsorship declaration)

common_mistakes.mistake: Applying for Temporary Authorisation to Stay (AST) from inside Luxembourg
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: You must apply from your home country, before travel and before entering Luxembourg.

common_mistakes.mistake: Missing the 90-day window after AST approval
common_mistakes.consequence: Potential issues with obtaining an entry visa or registering upon arrival.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Once your temporary authorization is granted, you only have 90 days to either get your entry visa or enter and register at your commune, so do not delay.

common_mistakes.consequence: A shortfall in required funds, leading to application rejection.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: The threshold is tied to the REVIS rate and adjusts periodically. Always verify the current amount on guichet.public.lu directly.

common_mistakes.consequence: Rejection of the application on technical grounds.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: If parents are financing your studies, the sponsorship declaration needs to cover a full academic year of costs (study, health, and return travel) and follow the specific legal format. Use the official template form.

common_mistakes.consequence: Legal issues related to your student status.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: The work limit is a monthly average (max 60 hours over a 4-week period), not a strict weekly cap. Track your total hours over the 4-week period.

common_mistakes.mistake: Missing the 30-day pre-expiry deadline for the post-study jobseeking permit
common_mistakes.consequence: Inability to transition to a job-seeking permit.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: This is a hard cutoff before your student permit expires, so plan well ahead of your graduation date.

common_mistakes.mistake: Forgetting to register at the local town hall (commune) within 3 working days of arrival
common_mistakes.consequence: Can jeopardize your final residence permit application.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Keep your flight ticket and lease agreement handy and register promptly.
If your documents are not in French, German, or English, they must be officially translated by a sworn translator.
